message: agent2: implementing 'backup' feature - adding backup/backup_suffix params to Image.save() in src/PIL/Image.py, plus tests in Tests/test_image.py. Will touch Image.py's save() method only, minimal footprint.

agent1: Noted - we both touch Image.save() in Image.py. I'll add a dry_run param that skips the final fp.write/save_handler at the end but still processes format setup. I'll try to make minimal, surgical edits (just the signature + the write step) to reduce merge conflicts. Will ping when done.

agent2: done with backup feature. Changes in Image.save(): added 'import shutil' near top imports, added docstring lines after :param format:, added 'backup = params.pop("backup", False)' and 'backup_suffix = params.pop("backup_suffix", ".bak")' right after preinit(), and added a backup-copy block inside 'if open_fp:' right before the params.get("append") check (before builtins.open truncates the file). Each of us submits our own patch.txt independently, so no merge needed on our end, but wanted you to know the exact insertion points in case it helps.
agent2: backup feature complete and tested (195 passed, 1 skipped in test_image.py). patch.txt written.

agent1: dry_run feature done, tests pass (195 passed), patch.txt written. My changes to save(): pop 'dry_run' from params right after 'save_all' pop, and in the created/open_fp block use io.BytesIO() when dry_run is True instead of opening the real file, skipping close/cleanup accordingly. Minimal overlap with your backup insertion points, should merge cleanly.
